content:
/poll Should Zulip go public? That is, should we:
Keep things how they are
Create a bot that one can message privately to receive a read only invite link
Create a bot that one can message privately to receive an ordinary invite link
Go completely public (no restrictions to becoming an ordinary member)
Go "read-only" public: allow anyone to view, but require one of the above options to become an ordinary member

Select options by clicking the boxes containing the vote counts. You can choose more than one option, so I'm going to arbitrarily say in advance that if the fifth option gets at least 60% of the votes of the most popular option, and that option is not the fourth option, then the fifth option shall be included as one of the new measures.
I shall also arbitrarily say that votes shall be tallied until 6pm CET on Tuesday (that is 17/11/2020). If anyone has a good argument for giving more time for voting, let me know.

Finally, if there are fewer than 150 votes tallied (counting multiplicities) by that deadline, the voting will be extended until the same time on Friday (20/11/2020) by default, to make sure we have enough folks' input. Voting will close at that stage. We'll implement whatever option(s) are chosen subsequently.
In Safari on MacOS Big Sur, you need to reload the page to see the change to your vote after clicking. (Other browsers may have the same problem)

[Mod] Morgan Rogers said:

I shall also arbitrarily say that votes shall be tallied until 6pm CET on Tuesday (that is 17/11/2020). If anyone has a good argument for giving more time for voting, let me know.

To confirm, the vote has been extended, although it seems I was rather optimistic about participation :sweat_smile:

At close of voting, the vote tallies are:
0 Keep things how they are
0 Create a bot that one can message privately to receive a read only invite link
21 Create a bot that one can message privately to receive an ordinary invite link
11 Go completely public (no restrictions to becoming an ordinary member)
19 Go "read-only" public: allow anyone to view, but require one of the above options to become an ordinary member
2 spoiled ballots :wink:

As such, the measures to implement are as follows:

  • A bot will be created that one can message to receive an ordinary invite link, which should subscribe members to all of the public streams on this Zulip forum.
  • The non-private streams on this forum will be made publicly readable.
  • Based on the participation in this poll, we will establish standard poll procedure for future polls.

Thanks for your input everyone!
@[Mod] Morgan Rogers when will the streams be publicly readable? Dmitri Pavlov was wanting to read something here, I believe, without making an account.
I believe it turned out that wasn't possible and that the best solution was to create an archive that was updated automatically. I'm not sure there was progress on this front since.
It's infinitely easier for him to get an account. It takes less than 2 minutes.
